text: The applicability of this utterance to the Christian Sacrifice needs no emphasizing. Its applicability to the conditions of the prophet’s own time has been the subject of much discussion; can it be supposed that he thought of heathen sacrifices as offered, unwittingly, to the true God? Or did he picture the Dispersion of the Jews, even then, as a world-wide phenomenon?
‘Gelt’, literally, ‘spoilt’; ‘entire’, literally, ‘male’. Some such contrast is evidently intended; cf. Lev. 22.24.
